head: 5e3523e
rule: ContactMesh.Core stays provider-neutral; no Google/Microsoft/Graph/Workspace references.
updatePolicy: Update this file after every completed functional/docs/test slice before final response or commit.

lastKnown.warnings: NU1900 package vulnerability feed unavailable in sandbox

bootstrap.instruction: New coding sessions should read AGENTS.md, then PROJECT_STATE.json, before continuing.

completed.note: Solution renamed/reorganized into src/tests/docs/samples/tools; OSS docs, gitignore, samples, provider shells.
Provider-neutral models/options/interfaces/sync planner/executor/merge shells.
Legacy GoogleUtils and Groups to Contacts migration sources preserved under ignored tools/migration paths.
Group visibility/member visibility and group mapping rules ported into Core; Google visibility mapper added.
Stale managed contacts delete only when no user-owned data remains; otherwise strip managed fields.
Managed duplicate contacts by primary email are merged and extras deleted.
Blank contacts and managed-email-only contacts can be pruned.
Included/excluded organization unit target eligibility rules, including =Ignore semantics.
Directory users map to managed MeshContact with work email, org fields, phones, labels, metadata.
Duplicate contact emails removed; provider-resolved send-as can become primary work email.
Policy-owned labels reconcile during merge while personal labels are preserved.
Visible groups can become managed contacts with group: source IDs.
All test projects use xunit.v3 3.2.2 and visualstudio runner 3.1.5; analyzer warning fixed.
Google provider now has tested contact-group label ownership metadata and create/update/delete reconciliation planning without live API calls.
Added AGENTS.md bootstrap prompt and PROJECT_STATE.json handoff ledger.
Installed local Codex skill at C:/Users/ZunairFayaz/.codex/skills/continue for /Continue-style task selection, verification, ledger update, and commit workflow.
GoogleContactBatchWriter reconciles desired contact labels through a Google contact-group label client port before contact writes.
Added a live People API HTTP client behind IGoogleContactGroupLabelClient, with delegated access-token seam and fake-handler tests for list/create/update/delete contact-group label calls.
Added delegated service-account access-token acquisition for Google People API calls, with fake token-source tests and explicit contacts scope docs/sample config.
Google People contacts now read through a live HTTP client, write create/update/delete through the batch writer, map planner metadata, and emit provider-neutral dry-run log entries.
Added provider-neutral run orchestration for directory users, group visibility/exclusions, group mappings, factories, planner execution, and wired CLI/Worker hosts to run it.
Expanded provider-neutral sync state contract for checkpoints and per-contact provider IDs/etags, with a JSON file-backed store and persistence tests.
Added provider-neutral sync run summary aggregation, structured dry-run operation log fields, shared CLI/Worker dry-run report output, and tests.
Added shared host configuration binding for ContactMesh, SyncRule, Google Workspace, and Microsoft365 options across CLI/Worker/Web, with binding tests.
Replaced the Web placeholder with a server-rendered settings overview for runtime, rules, and provider options, with renderer tests for binding display, encoding, and secret masking.
Added Microsoft Graph client-credentials token request normalization and HTTP token source through MicrosoftGraphClientFactory, with fake-source and fake-handler tests.
Added a paged Microsoft Graph users client, mapped Graph users to MeshUser, and wired Microsoft365 host directory reads with fake-handler/provider tests.
Added a live Graph contacts client/provider for Outlook contact list/create/update/delete, source-id extended properties, category label mapping, host wiring, and fake-handler/provider tests.

Added live Graph group and transitive membership reads, mapped group users/org contacts into provider-neutral groups/contacts, and wired Microsoft365 host group reads with fake-handler/provider tests.

Updated README to reflect current provider/host state, .NET 8 build and test commands, config flow, CLI/Worker/Web run steps, provider setup notes, pending Docker hosted worker mode, and local appsettings ignore coverage.

Ran a live Microsoft365 dry-run with app credentials: Graph auth/users/groups/contact reads succeeded for mailbox-enabled targets; 297 targets produced 81,437 planned creates with writes skipped, and 24 targets reported MailboxNotEnabledForRESTAPI without aborting the run.

Added provider-neutral TargetUsers scoping so Microsoft365 dry-runs can limit sync recipients while preserving eligible directory users as source contacts; documented and surfaced the setting in samples/config/UI tests.

Added detailed Web settings descriptions for runtime, sync rules, provider credentials/scopes, dry-run safety, TargetUsers live-test scoping, and credential masking; renderer coverage now checks the guidance.

Web settings now resolves repo-root appsettings.local.json from project launches, shows the absolute config path/load status, renders editable settings fields, and saves JSON back to the ignored local config while preserving masked secrets.
Added an opt-in live-provider dry-run test project with explicit environment gating, secret-free config path injection, DryRun and TargetUsers safety assertions, and docs for secure execution.

Added MainContactsGroupEmail source scoping and MainContactsGroupLabel directory labeling so managed directory contacts can come from a configured group, including nested groups, without limiting sync recipients.
Removed the misspelled main contacts group label compatibility option and kept MainContactsGroupLabel as the only configuration spelling.
SyncPlanner now adopts a unique existing contact with a matching email when source-id metadata is absent, so personal/company-email contacts plan as updates instead of duplicate creates.

Configured managed email domains now flow into sync planning, and unmatched unmanaged company-domain contacts are cleaned as stale contacts so empty company contacts delete while notes/user-owned details are preserved.
Dry-run cleanup updates now identify the original contact when cleaned fields are blank and stale cleanup reasons list which user-owned detail categories prevented deletion.
Stale cleanup dry-run reasons now include preserved user-owned values, including metadata key/value pairs, emails, phones, labels, and notes.
Microsoft365 host planning now treats Graph contactId/changeKey metadata as managed cleanup fields so those provider bookkeeping values do not prevent stale contact deletion.
Stale cleanup now treats counted notes made only of managed phone types, such as workMobile migration blocks, as managed data instead of user-owned notes.

Ported legacy groupsToSyncByGroup behavior as provider-neutral GroupsToSyncByGroup: configured container groups expand direct group members into managed group-email contacts, including member-only group records, with config/docs/Web/test coverage.
Added provider-neutral ContactMesh.DisableDeletes so live runs preserve planned delete reporting while skipping contact delete writes, with config/docs/Web/test coverage.
ContactSyncOrchestrator now caches group contact reads per run so shared visible groups are not re-read for every target user; core coverage asserts one provider read across multiple targets.
Added configurable provider-neutral group contact display-name prefix with + default, surfaced in config/docs/Web settings, and covered by core/hosting/web tests.
Managed group contact display names now hyphenate whitespace before applying the group prefix, matching legacy names such as +833-Chestnut-Street-(833C) Group.
GroupsToSyncByGroup-expanded member groups now apply their group labels to nested directory contacts, porting the legacy Labels -> Location -> Branch contact-label behavior.
ContactSyncOrchestrator now emits provider-neutral per-target progress events, and CLI/Worker stream target start/completion/failure lines during sync runs.

Dry-run output now prints planned contact labels and provider-neutral source-rule details so visible group contacts, visible group member contacts, directory contacts, and GroupsToSyncByGroup contacts can be traced.
Restricted group-derived label creation to GroupsToSyncByGroup member groups; ordinary visible groups no longer create labels, and label groups now use display name before email/id fallback.

Planner is now built at runtime inside RunAsync after groups resolve; both ContactMergeEngine and StaleContactCleanupEngine receive all GroupsToSyncByGroup group label values (display name + email + id) as managed labels, so stale ID and email labels written by previous syncs are stripped on the next update. ContactMeshHostFactory passes provider-specific managed metadata keys instead of an injected planner.

Added ForceResetLabels bool to ContactMeshOptions and ContactMergeOptions. When true, ContactMergeEngine replaces all existing labels with desired labels instead of reconciling, clearing any stale accumulated labels in one run. Surfaced in web settings, docs, and covered by merge engine test.
Dry-run update lines now include a Labels removed: line when labels are stripped from an existing contact, showing the before/after diff.

SyncPlanner now accepts a ContactEmailPolicyEngine and applies it to every planned contact (creates and updates). ContactEmailPolicyEngine promotes managed-domain emails from 'other' type to 'work' and deduplicates same-address entries. Wired up in ContactSyncOrchestrator.CreatePlanner using options.ManagedEmailDomains.

Fixed SyncPlanner.DictionariesEqual to only check that all existing (provider-persisted) metadata keys are present in merged with correct values. Previously the count equality check treated ephemeral source metadata keys (sourceRule, userId, organizationUnit, groupId, etc.) as changed, triggering spurious Update operations on every run because those keys are never written to or read back from any provider. After the fix, syncs stabilize to NoChange on the second run.

ContactMergeEngine now preserves the provider's stored phone number string when source and existing normalize to the same digits and have the same type. Prevents infinite Update loops when providers (e.g. Microsoft Graph) reformat number strings on storage. Three new ContactMergeEngineTests cover the behaviour.

Added Microsoft365:GroupTypes list setting to load only selected group types (Microsoft365, MailEnabledSecurity, Distribution). Empty = all types. MicrosoftGroupType enum and GetGroupType classifier added to MicrosoftGroupMapper; MicrosoftGroupProvider filters before fetching members; ContactMeshHostFactory passes options.GroupTypes; surfaced in web settings and sample config with 11 new tests.

GroupsToSyncByGroup now uses a 3-level hierarchy: L1=container, L2=label groups (display name becomes the contact label), L3=contact groups (become the group-email contacts). Directory users inside L3 groups are not labeled. Removed ContainsUserMember/MatchesUser dead code. Introduced private GroupContactSource(Group, LabelName) record. Tests updated.

Fixed perpetual stale-contact update loop: (1) OperationalMetadataKeys added to StaleContactCleanupOptions so provider write-essential keys (contactId, changeKey) are preserved in the cleaned contact but excluded from HasUserOwnedData — previously they were stripped, making every Graph PATCH a no-op. (2) ForceResetLabels added to StaleContactCleanupOptions and propagated from ContactMeshOptions so app-created labels from now-removed groups are cleared, allowing group contacts with no other user-owned data to be deleted. (3) Name fields (DisplayName, GivenName, etc.) are no longer nulled in cleanup because PATCH omits nulls and Graph keeps old values, causing perpetual mismatch. (4) AreEquivalent check added to both stale loops in SyncPlanner so a contact that is already in the cleaned state emits NoChange instead of a spurious Update. ContactMeshHostFactory now passes contactId/changeKey as additionalOperationalMetadataKeys instead of additionalManagedMetadataKeys.

Added ContactMesh.ForceDeduplicatePhones as an opt-in one-time cleanup switch. When enabled, ContactMergeEngine collapses duplicate managed phone numbers across different phone types while default sync behavior still preserves those distinct fields. Surfaced in Web settings, samples, docs, and covered by core/hosting/web tests.

Added ContactMesh.ForceNormalizeEmailTypes as an opt-in one-time cleanup switch. When enabled, ContactEmailPolicyEngine promotes the selected email to primary work email so legacy managed contacts stop showing organization addresses under Other email. Surfaced in Web settings, samples, docs, and covered by core/hosting/web tests.

Microsoft Graph contacts now select, read, and write primaryEmailAddress/secondaryEmailAddress/tertiaryEmailAddress instead of only the generic emailAddresses collection. Generic emailAddresses remains a read fallback. This should place managed org email into Outlook's primary email slot instead of Other email.

Microsoft Graph contact PATCH/POST payloads now explicitly serialize null primary/secondary/tertiary email slots and mobilePhone when absent, matching Graph's documented clear-by-null update behavior. This lets one-email/one-phone managed updates clear stale Outlook Other email and mobile fields instead of preserving omitted values.

Microsoft Graph contact reads now capture the extra emailAddresses[].type value when Graph returns it and map it back to the matching primary/secondary/tertiary slot address so Core can compare email address, primary flag, and type more accurately without adding a Microsoft Graph SDK dependency.

Added a focused Microsoft365 CLI diagnostic command, m365-contact-email-slot, that reads exactly one contact by email or Graph id and optionally performs a two-step live reset: clear primary/secondary/tertiary email slots, then write one primary work email. Live probe of zftest@mhphope.org in zfayaz@mhphope.org showed Graph returns a primaryEmailAddress plus a mirrored emailAddresses[] entry even after reset; attempting to write emailAddresses[].type=work to v1 failed because Graph rejects type on microsoft.graph.completeEmailAddress.

Added Microsoft365.ContactDiagnostic config for the m365-contact-email-slot command so focused contact reports/resets can be run from config. Supports User, Contacts, ContactIds, WorkEmail, and Apply, with CLI args still overriding config. Live dry-run for target mailbox zfayaz@mhphope.org and contact zfadmin@mhphope.org found one match, with Graph reporting the address in secondaryEmailAddress plus a mirrored emailAddresses[] entry.

Fixed m365-contact-email-slot so blank ContactDiagnostic config values, such as WorkEmail, are treated as missing values and do not override CLI/contact fallback values. Live report after moving zfadmin@mhphope.org back to Other in Outlook showed Graph now stores it in secondaryEmailAddress with primaryEmailAddress null.

Added beta-only typed email diagnostic support to m365-contact-email-slot via --beta-email-type. It PATCHes /beta/users/{user}/contacts/{contactId} with typed emailAddresses[] and reads beta back with id/displayName/emailAddresses. Live test for target zfayaz@mhphope.org/contact zfadmin@mhphope.org: v1 reset restored primaryEmailAddress; beta PATCH accepted type=work; beta GET returned emailAddresses[] type=work; v1 subsequently projected the contact as primaryEmailAddress with no type.

Added per-run dual CSV audit logs (detail + summary) via provider-neutral RunAuditWriter, and success/failure email notifications via Microsoft Graph sendMail. New ContactMesh:AuditLog and ContactMesh:Notifications options bind through host config; runs route through ContactSyncRunPipeline used by CLI/Worker; dispatcher skips on dry-run (including early dry-run failures), missing sender, or empty recipients; failure mails attach summary+detail CSVs truncated to MaxAttachmentBytes. Docs added at docs/audit-and-notifications.md and referenced from configuration.md; samples updated for Microsoft365 (enabled) and Google (sender stays null). 14 new tests in Core/Microsoft365.

Settings dashboard now surfaces ContactMesh:Notifications settings and provides a Send test email action at /settings/test-email. The test uses current form values without saving, sends one Microsoft Graph sendMail message to distinct success+failure recipients, and renders sent/skipped/failed notice feedback. Hosting has a NotificationTestEmailService with fake-handler coverage; Web renderer/form tests cover notification fields and persistence.

Settings dashboard notification test panel now tells admins the Microsoft 365 app registration needs Microsoft Graph Application permission Mail.Send with admin consent, not Delegated permission, and notes Exchange Application Access Policy sender scope. MicrosoftGraphMailNotificationSender now enriches 403 ErrorAccessDenied sendMail failures with the same guidance; docs and tests updated.

AuditLog section added to Web settings UI with editable Enabled, Directory, IncludeNoChange, and IncludeDryRunPlannedAsWrites fields; nav link added between Notifications and Rules. SettingsFormModel.FromForm now binds ContactMesh.AuditLog.* so saves no longer silently reset Directory to default; Coalesce helper preserves blank-vs-missing distinction.

Wired IncludeDryRunPlannedAsWrites in RunAuditWriter.ResolveStatus: when true and dry-run, operation rows use Status=Written instead of Planned so downstream dashboards count them the same as live writes. Previously the option was declared but never read. docs/audit-and-notifications.md updated to describe actual behaviour.

RunAuditWriter now skips creating the -detail.csv when there are no rows to write (all operations excluded by IncludeNoChange filter, no warnings, no errors). DetailCsvPath is empty string in that case. Pipeline log line and notification email body both omit the detail reference. New test Writer_Skips_Detail_File_When_No_Rows_To_Write added; total 227 pass, 1 skipped.
MicrosoftDirectoryMapper now marks UserType != Member (e.g. Guest) as IsSuspended=true so guest accounts with no Exchange mailbox are excluded from targets and source contacts.

ContactSyncOrchestrator now resolves GlobalUserGroups members and concatenates with TargetUsers as effectiveTargetUsers before passing to SyncRuleEngine, so GlobalUserGroups actually limits the sync target scope.
MicrosoftGroupProvider.IsAllowedGroupType now treats 'Security' config value as matching MailEnabledSecurity so mail-enabled security groups load when GroupTypes includes Security.
MicrosoftGraphContactClient.ListAsync returns empty contacts for MailboxNotEnabledForRESTAPI 404 instead of throwing, so service accounts and on-prem mailboxes are not counted as target failures.

ContactSyncOrchestrator now emits a RunStarted progress event before the per-target loop. The event carries TargetCount and a Message describing the active scope path (TargetUsers, GlobalUserGroups with group names, or 'all eligible directory users'), including a warning when a GlobalUserGroups group has no resolved members. SyncProgressFormatter renders 'Scope: {Message} ({N} targets)'. 241 tests pass, 1 skipped.

Audit detail and summary CSVs now include TargetUserEmail immediately after TargetUserId. SyncResult carries TargetUserEmail from SyncTarget.UserEmail via SyncExecutor and target failure results; RunAuditWriter writes the email for operation, warning/error, and target summary rows while run summary rows leave target fields blank. Docs and audit/executor tests updated.

MicrosoftClientCredentialsAccessTokenProvider now caches client-credentials access tokens for 50 minutes with a 5-minute refresh skew and synchronizes refreshes, reducing repeated login.microsoftonline.com token requests across multi-target runs. Added provider caching test.

Settings dashboard Runtime provider now renders as a dropdown with Microsoft365, Google, and Scaffolded options while preserving an unknown current provider value as a selected option. Renderer tests cover selected known, default scaffolded, and custom provider values.

GroupsToSyncByGroup labels/categories now apply GroupContactPrefix, while MainContactsGroupLabel remains unprefixed. Planner managed-label ownership includes both prefixed desired labels and previous raw labels so old unprefixed managed categories are removed on update. Docs, Web setting copy, and core orchestrator tests updated; build passed and 243 tests passed, 1 skipped.

Microsoft365 GroupTypes in the Web settings UI now renders as a checklist dropdown with Microsoft365, MailEnabledSecurity, Distribution, and Security options, preserves custom configured values, saves repeated checkbox values as a JSON array, and uses a wider responsive provider layout so the picker is not cramped. Build passed and 244 tests passed, 1 skipped.

Replaced singular MainContactsGroupEmail with MainContactsGroupEmails list config. ContactSyncOrchestrator now unions resolved members from all listed main contact groups, Web settings renders/saves the list, and docs/samples/tests use the plural setting only. Build passed with NU1900 sandbox warnings; tests passed 239, skipped 1.

ContactSyncOrchestrator now applies MainContactsGroupLabel (or default Directory label) to every managed desired contact type, including group contacts and visible group member contacts. Updated sync rules docs and expanded orchestrator assertions; ContactMesh.Core.Tests passed 135/135.

Hid dormant ScopedGroupRoots from the Web settings rules UI while preserving existing configured values during Web saves and test-email form binding. ContactMesh.Web.Tests passed 7/7. Full solution build was blocked by locked ContactMesh.Cli output DLLs from running dotnet processes.

Phone normalization now treats +12675073489, 12675073489, 2675073489, and 267-507-3489 as the same U.S. number and formats new/cleanup merge output as 267-507-3489. Covered by normalizer, duplicate detector, merge engine, and duplicate consolidation tests. Build passed and 256 tests passed, 1 skipped.

Added provider-neutral alternate email and match-email support so alias drift does not miss target/source/group visibility matches; Microsoft365 now reads proxyAddresses, prefers primary SMTP proxies, surfaces mismatch warnings, keeps aliases as match hints instead of written contact emails, and routes warning-only live notifications to failure recipients. Build passed and 264 tests passed, 1 skipped.

ContactSyncOrchestrator now collects alias drift run warnings only after eligibility filtering and only for users/group members with identity emails in ContactMesh.ManagedEmailDomains, suppressing external guest #EXT# noise while keeping managed-domain mismatch alerts. Build passed and 265 tests passed, 1 skipped.

Disabled non-external users can remain managed source contacts while staying excluded as sync targets; external users are separately flagged and excluded. Managed org fields now clear from blank directory values, old Directory/-Directory labels are treated as managed during MainContactsGroupLabel migration, and Microsoft Graph PATCH payloads clear company/department/jobTitle with nulls. Build passed and 269 tests passed, 1 skipped.

Strengthened ContactSyncOrchestrator coverage so a disabled user inside MainContactsGroupEmails updates an existing managed contact when directory company/department/jobTitle values change. Build passed and 269 tests passed, 1 skipped.

Added label-only regression coverage so a disabled user inside MainContactsGroupEmails still updates an existing managed contact when the managed directory label changes, preserving personal labels while replacing stale managed labels. Build passed and 270 tests passed, 1 skipped.

Added planner coverage for unmanaged existing contacts whose email is in a managed domain: stale legacy -Directory labels are removed while personal labels are preserved. Live read-only Graph scan of configured target zfayaz@mhphope.org found 207 managed contacts with Directory-like categories, all on -MHP Directory and zero exact -Directory contact categories across 7 contact folders; Outlook master category lookup was denied by current app permissions. Build passed and 271 tests passed, 1 skipped.

Microsoft Graph contact reads now traverse contact folders recursively, preserve contactFolderId for folder-scoped update/delete endpoints, surface legacy managed-looking folder names such as -Directory as labels for cleanup, and planner stale detection now treats managed labels as stale markers even without managed email slots. Duplicate same-managed-email contacts are merged into the source-backed keeper and extras deleted. Live dry-run for zfayaz@mhphope.org now plans 0 create, 0 update, 1 delete, 207 unchanged; the delete is the folder-only stale Krystle Comer row. Build passed and 276 tests passed, 1 skipped.

Fixed repeated Microsoft365 category cleanup planning by mapping managed-looking contact folder names (for example -Directory) into provider metadata instead of normal labels. Core now supports provider-neutral managed marker metadata for stale cleanup, preserving marker metadata as operational so folder-only stale contacts can still be detected without causing source-backed contacts to plan non-persistable label removals. Reproduced live dry-run before fix at 0 create, 114 update, 0 delete; reran after fix at 0 create, 0 update, 0 delete, 39,522 unchanged. Build passed with NU1900 sandbox feed warnings; tests passed 278, skipped 1.

Committed safe Visual Studio project launch/publish profile files for CLI/Core publish workflows, excluding user-specific .pubxml.user history files. Prior build passed with NU1900 sandbox feed warnings; tests passed 278, skipped 1.

pending.note: Sample compose exists; production Docker/hosted worker mode not implemented.

blocked.note: Remote origin is configured but user said push later.
